summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering police department personnel matters, adoption of a new police patch, and a resolution to petition for the vacation of Broadway Street. Officer Beckelheimer was promoted to Sergeant, an officer's resignation was accepted, unpaid leave was approved for a new officer, and the board approved Resolution R0112021 to vacate three sections of Broadway Street at the request of a property owner.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for April 28, 2021 through May 11, 2021
Approved unpaid leave of absence for Officer Tyler Smith June 6-12, 2021
Accepted resignation of Gareth L. Carter effective April 29, 2021
Approved promotion of Officer James Beckelheimer to Sergeant effective May 23, 2021
Approved Resolution R0112021 to petition to vacate Broadway Street (three sections)
action_items: Township Clerk to file copy of Resolution R0112021 with Board of County Commissioners and certify copy to County Engineer
Branding and logo discussion scheduled for May 18 meeting
Township Association meeting on May 20 to discuss CARES Act distribution rules
Next CPAAA class to be held in August
key_amounts: Broadway Street vacation parcels: 0.3776 acres, 0.2955 acres, and 0.0821 acres
notable_topics: Police department personnel: resignation, unpaid leave, and promotion to Sergeant
Adoption of new police patch design with American flag motif by Tyler Bailey at Wage of War Apparel
Resolution R0112021 to vacate three sections of Broadway Street per property owner Charles Thomas request
Grand opening of Loveland Express at 132 and Deerfield (12-acre reconstructed site)
Federal CARES Act distribution rules and upcoming Zoom meeting
New police department floor installation completed
Fire department new recruit orientation completion
attendees: Harry Holford Jr. (Planning and Zoning Director)
Tyler Bailey (mentioned, Wage of War Apparel)
notes: Vice Chair Lisa Allen participated remotely via Zoom while traveling with family and expected to return Thursday. Steve (likely the regular tech/admin person) was absent due to classes at Xavier, so Director Holbrook filled in on the computer. The board noted humorously that the new gas station at 132 and Deerfield was named 'Loveland Express' instead of 'Goshen Express.' Meeting adjourned at 7:30 PM.
